data.title: Injective (INJ) introduces AI toolkit with 11 open-source tools for autonomous trading agents to trade futures and deploy contracts.
data.description: Injective (INJ) has recently launched 11 open-source tools that allow AI agents to engage in trading perpetual futures, initiate contracts, and facilitate the bridging of assets by following natural language commands. These tools aim to enhance the efficiency and accessibility of these processes within the cryptocurrency space.

data.title: MoonPay Agents Introduces the First AI Agent Secured by a Ledger Signer
data.description: Autonomous AI agents are now able to engage in crypto trading, with each transaction being verified on a physical Ledger™ signer for enhanced security where private keys remain secure. The announcement was made in New York on March 13, 2026 by MoonPay, a prominent player in global crypto payments. MoonPay has introduced native Ledger signer support, allowing for securely conducted transactions within the crypto market.

data.title: FDA Broadens Access To GSK's Respiratory Syncytial Virus Shot For Younger High-Risk Adults
data.description: The FDA recently expanded the age approval for GSK plc's respiratory syncytial virus vaccine, Arexvy, to include adults between 18 and 49 years old who are at risk of lower respiratory tract disease from RSV. This vaccine was initially for those aged 60 and above, as well as for individuals aged 50-59 at risk of complications. Pregnant individuals are not eligible for this vaccine. Sanjay Gurunathan, GSK's head of vaccines and infectious diseases research and development, highlighted the importance of this expansion, citing the unmet medical needs of younger adults with underlying health conditions. This decision aims to protect at-risk individuals from severe RSV disease and alleviate healthcare system burdens.

data.title: Rubrik Posts Strong Q4, But 'Rarified Air' Isn't Enough To Lift Shares In Soft Software Market
data.description: Rubrik Inc (NYSE: RBRK) experienced a decline in shares at the start of trading despite posting positive fiscal fourth-quarter results. Analysts provided their insights on the situation: Dan Ives from Wedbush maintained an Outperform rating but reduced the price target to $90. Jonathan Ruykhaver of Cantor Fitzgerald affirmed an Overweight rating with a $85 price target. Meanwhile, John DiFucci from Guggenheim Securities reiterated a Buy rating and a price target of $110. According to Wedbush, Rubrik surpassed expectations with significant growth in revenue, subscription ARR, and margins. Subscription ARR saw a 34% year-on-year increase to $1.46 billion, exceeding the estimated $1.44 billion. For further details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Campbell’s Appoints Joshua Levine Chief Investor Relations Officer
data.description: Camden, N.J. - Campbell’s Company (NASDAQ: CPB) has appointed Joshua Levine as Chief Investor Relations Officer, effective March 18, 2026. Levine will directly report to Chief Financial Officer Todd Cunfer. In his new role, Levine will be responsible for managing the Investor Relations function at Campbell’s, conducting interactions with the investment community, and ensuring clear and consistent communication with shareholders. He takes over from Rebecca Gardy, who recently retired from the position.

data.title: [MWC 2026] GlobalData vydává bílou knihu o vývoji hlasových technologií v éře umělé inteligence
data.description: In Barcelona, Spain on March 13, 2026, at the MWC 2026 trade show, Andy Hicks, the lead analyst at GlobalData, presented a white paper titled Reinventing Voice: A Converged, AI-Enabled, and Multimodal Voice Core for the Next Generation of Telecommunications. This white paper emphasizes the revolutionary advancements in voice technology for telecom, targeting the future with innovation.
